domain_name: Lower Leg — Achilles, Shin Splints, Calf
search_terms_used: Achilles tendinopathy treatment network meta-analysis pubmed
Achilles tendon loading rehabilitation runners systematic review pubmed
heavy slow resistance eccentric Achilles tendinopathy RCT pubmed
medial tibial stress syndrome runners risk factors meta-analysis pubmed
shin splints MTSS running prevention treatment pubmed
calf strain risk factors runners systematic review pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage of all three lower-leg pathologies. Achilles: network meta-analysis (van der Vlist 2021), loading SR (Maetz 2023), HSR vs eccentric RCT (Beyer 2015), conservative management (Silbernagel 2020). MTSS: risk factor meta (Reinking 2017), runner-specific meta (Newman 2013), muscle anatomy (Mattock 2021). Calf: risk factor SR (Green 2017). Good mix of foundational and recent evidence.

papers.title: Which treatment is most effective for patients with Achilles tendinopathy? A living systematic review with network meta-analysis of 29 randomised controlled trials
papers.notes: Living network meta-analysis of 29 RCTs comparing treatments for Achilles tendinopathy, finding exercise-based treatments superior to wait-and-see.

papers.title: Exercise-Based Treatment for Achilles Tendinopathy: A Systematic Review of the Characteristics of the Therapeutic Exercise Programs
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed exercise program characteristics (type, dosage, progression) for Achilles tendinopathy treatment across RCTs.

papers.title: Heavy Slow Resistance Versus Eccentric Training as Treatment for Achilles Tendinopathy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
papers.notes: RCT comparing heavy slow resistance (HSR) vs Alfredson eccentric protocol for midportion Achilles tendinopathy. Both effective at 12 weeks; HSR showed higher patient satisfaction.

papers.title: Current Clinical Concepts: Conservative Management of Achilles Tendinopathy
papers.notes: Evidence-based clinical concepts review for conservative Achilles tendinopathy management including loading progression, pain monitoring, and return-to-sport criteria.

papers.title: Medial Tibial Stress Syndrome in Active Individuals: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Risk Factors
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed prospective studies to identify risk factors for MTSS including prior history, female sex, increased BMI, navicular drop, and hip ROM.

papers.title: Risk factors associated with medial tibial stress syndrome in runners: a systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.journal: Open Access Journal of Sports Medicine
papers.notes: Runner-specific meta-analysis of MTSS risk factors, identifying prior MTSS history, female sex, higher BMI, navicular drop, and ankle plantarflexion ROM as significant predictors.

papers.title: Calf muscle strain injuries in sport: a systematic review of risk factors for injury
papers.notes: Systematic review of risk factors for calf muscle strain injuries in sport, identifying older age, previous injury, and reduced ankle dorsiflexion as key factors.

papers.title: A systematic review and meta-analysis of the association between lower leg muscle morphology and medial tibial stress syndrome
papers.journal: Journal of Foot and Ankle Research
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed associations between lower leg muscle morphology (cross-sectional area, lean mass) and MTSS risk, finding smaller calf muscle size associated with MTSS.
